title: Peruvian-Style Aerated Mussels Brochettes
description: A malty, aerated twist on traditional Peruvian mussels, featuring nuka-pickle and a low-calorie-friendly twist. Perfect for a sick-day meal or snack.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine mussels, nuka-pickle, cilantro, ginger, lime juice, garlic, cumin, chili flakes, and black pepper.
Marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Thread 4-6 mussels onto each copper-mug brochette, leaving a small space between each mussel.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add brochettes and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until mussels are heated through and slightly charred.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Garnish with a sprinkle of nuka-pickle for added flavor and texture.
For an extra crispy exterior, broil brochettes for an additional 1-2 minutes after cooking.
Serve hot, with additional lime juice and chili flakes on the side for added flavor.
Brochettes can be made ahead and refrigerated for up to 24 hours before cooking.
Cooked brochettes can be refrigerated for up to 3 days and reheated in the microwave or oven before serving.
